items.title: Meta Platforms’ Cloud Push: Growth Opportunity Versus AI Concerns
items.summary: Meta Platforms' plan to sell excess compute capacity to third parties boosted its stock 8.8% on July 1. While this cloud push could generate significant new revenue and reduce future CapEx spending concerns, it raises questions about Meta's AI competitiveness and contradicts CFO statements about underestimated compute needs. The company has yet to provide concrete details on the initiative.

items.title: 3 AI Stocks That Could Outperform the S&P 500 for Years to Come
items.summary: The article highlights three AI-focused tech companies positioned to outperform the S&P 500: Micron Technology, benefiting from surging demand for memory processors in data centers; Alphabet, which has caught up in AI chatbots with Gemini and is generating significant revenue from AI services; and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing, which dominates global processor manufacturing with 90% market share in advanced AI chips.

items.title: Where Will Alphabet Stock Be in 5 Years?
items.summary: Alphabet's stock is positioned for strong growth over the next five years, driven by AI leadership with Google Gemini, Google Cloud's 63% revenue growth, and the emerging potential of autonomous driving platform Waymo. Despite heavy capital expenditure of $175-185 billion annually, the company's 27 P/E ratio remains below the S&P 500 average and most Magnificent Seven peers, suggesting room for further appreciation.

items.title: Password Management Market to Reach $42.21 Billion by 2035 as Cyberattacks and Identity Security Risks Drive Enterprise Adoption | Report by SNS Insider
items.summary: The global password management market is projected to grow from $5.55 billion in 2025 to $42.21 billion by 2035, driven by rising cyberattacks, data breaches, remote work expansion, and regulatory compliance requirements. The U.S. market is expected to reach $12.53 billion while Europe reaches $2.63 billion by 2035. Large enterprises currently dominate the market, with BFSI and healthcare sectors leading adoption.

items.title: SpaceX Won't Use Traditional Wire Services to Share Financial Results. What That Means for Investors.
items.summary: SpaceX announced it will share quarterly and annual financial results through its investor relations website and X account instead of traditional wire services like Business Wire or PR Newswire. While the company remains compliant with SEC requirements, this strategy places greater responsibility on investors to actively monitor SpaceX's disclosures. The move comes as SpaceX operates an increasingly complex business spanning satellite internet (Starlink), AI infrastructure, and space launch services.

items.title: CureWise Founder and CEO Steve Brown to Speak at Aspen Ideas: Health 2026 and Launch AI-Powered Precision Oncology Platform
items.summary: CureWise, an AI-powered precision oncology platform, officially launched at Aspen Ideas: Health 2026. Founded by Steve Brown following his rare cancer diagnosis, the platform helps cancer patients understand their disease, explore personalized treatment options, and advocate for targeted therapies by securely integrating medical records and leveraging multiple AI models to identify relevant clinical trials.

items.title: Musk, Misunderstanding And SpaceX's Next Chapter: An Insider's View Of The Space Giant
items.summary: SpaceX President Gwynne Shotwell discussed the company's IPO pricing at $135 per share on IPO day, emphasizing long-term investor focus and retail participation (33% allocation). She highlighted major growth opportunities in Starship, Starlink, and Grok, positioned SpaceX as an infrastructure company building data centers on ground and in space, and addressed the $28.5 trillion total addressable market driven by AI, robotics, and autonomous vehicles.
